summary: At frozen HEAD a9109350, the current-head verification and acceptance adjudication evidence substantiate the implementation and fail-closed typed-stop behavior, but the stored current pr-prepare still does not support PR readiness after excluding this review and the pending spine:done_evidence adjudication. During this inspection the worktree advanced to 63ba8d1d, so an a910 strict-HEAD gate review cannot be used as current-HEAD PR evidence.
inspection_summary: Inspected the generated gate_evidence request; a910-bound verification records and status artifacts; stored and freshly generated bounded/full pr-prepare projections; prior strict-head review/lifecycle metadata; acceptance and judgment adjudication events; the real guarded Run run-20260724T130916Z-7c409e4b; a910 conformance output; and the selected route reconciliation implementation plus its unit/E2E coverage. No test suite was rerun.
inspection_inputs: git rev-parse HEAD; git log --oneline a9109350..HEAD; git diff --name-status a9109350..HEAD
judgment_delta: Initial concern: historical gate reviews and adjudications could have been stale after the reconciliation change. Final assessment: all five verification records are content-surface-bound to a9109350 with clean git context; focused E2E reports 124/124 and the recorded full suite 2028/2028, while AC-1 through AC-9 each have current-head demonstrated adjudication.

Initial concern: the real Run might incorrectly be treated as successful E2E. Final assessment: it is correctly persisted as an orphaned-provider failure followed by a max_iterations_exceeded typed stop with recovery command; this supports fail-closed behavior only, not a PR-ready success claim.

Initial concern: excluding the active gate review and pending spine:done_evidence could make the PR otherwise ready. Final assessment: no. The current full pr-prepare also reports gate:common_judgment_spine missing current_reality and failure_modes and gate:responsibility_authority missing required evidence; neither is removed by excluding this review or spine:done_evidence.

Frozen-head binding change: inspection began at a9109350819af99df22448d6ed8bd75adf611e36, then HEAD advanced to 63ba8d1dc49d301ffa9ad41890c5708e7677b14d with committed prepare-artifacts projections. The prior gate_evidence role is strict-head-bound, so no a910 verdict is current-HEAD evidence.

findings.detail: The a910 current pr-prepare projection has overall_status=needs_verification and ready_for_pr_create=false. Beyond the active gate/release review lifecycles and pending judgment spine:done_evidence, it reports gate:common_judgment_spine needs evidence for current_reality and failure_modes and gate:responsibility_authority needs one required evidence item. Do not claim PR readiness until a fresh pr-prepare on the final HEAD resolves or explicitly continues to report these gates. Source: .vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/pr-prepare.json.

The worktree advanced from frozen a9109350 to 63ba8d1d during this review. The existing gate_evidence result is strict_head and bound to 3ba30980; this a910 review is likewise only a frozen-head assessment. A current-HEAD gate_evidence review must be authorized, closed, recorded, and followed by pr-prepare after the tree is finalized. Source: git log a9109350..63ba8d1d and .vibepro/reviews/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/gate/review-result-gate_evidence.json.

Each a910 verification record is clean and content-surface-bound, but carries managed_worktree_locality=needs_review because the preferred managed branch name differs from the actual reconcile-routing branch. The configured mode is preferred rather than required, so this is not a bypass or pass condition; retain it as a visible warning when regenerating evidence. Source: .vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/verification-evidence.json.
